sections.paragraph:
κατατρίβω fut. 3 sg. κατατρίψει Da 7:25; 2 aor. pass. κατετρίβην (in var. mngs. since Theognis 55; Aristoph., Pla., X.; ins, pap, LXX; Philo, Leg. Alleg. 2, 98; Jos., Bell. 4, 127) ‘rub down’ or ‘away’ hence wear out (s. τρίβω; Pla., Phd. 87c; Dt 8:4; cp. PPetr II, 32 [1] 21f [III b.c.]; PSI 527, 10 [III b.c.]) ἱμάτια κατατρίβεται clothes wear out AcPl Ha 2, 25; 9, 9f (restored).

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

var. var. = various(ly)

Theognis Theognis , VI b.c.—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

PPetr PPetr = The Flinders Petrie Papyri—List 4

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 528
